title: Sedum pachucense
taxonomy: Sedum pachucense is a species of the plant genus Sedum. It belongs to the family Crassulaceae. The Crassulaceae family is part of the order Saxifragales. This classification places Sedum pachucense within the larger group of flowering plants known as angiosperms. The species' classification is further detailed within the kingdom Plantae.

intro: Sedum pachucense, also known as Devil's backbone, is a succulent plant species native to Mexico, characterized by its unique, tree-like structure and ability to thrive in arid environments. The plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine to treat various health issues, including fever, rheumatism, and gastrointestinal problems, due to its purported health benefits, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The bioactive constituents of Sedum pachucense include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which are believed to contribute to its medicinal properties. Sedum pachucense is typically used in herbal preparations such as teas, infusions, and tinctures, as well as in traditional remedies like salves and ointments.
